column33: Comments

It was very difficult for me to choose Heroes for this setup. I went with the Draw cards mechanism provided by Banshee and Psylocke to take advantage of Gamora Artifacts, and Kitty was added in the mix to ko unwanted cards. Second hand mostly cleaned up HQ, but managed to get a few vps with undercover from special officers.

This was rough. Lost the first round by the scheme. Would have had more escapes on the 2nd turn if it wasn't for bonding. My hope of using Groot/Venompool for bystanders kinda worked, but with little KO power this took way too many turns to finish the game.
Ar first glance MicroDisney wants to watch the world burn. But the setup was actually fun and very well put together. It seems impossible, but is actually winnable.

I appreciated the creativity of the setup, and enjoyed the idea of theory crafting heroes for a super unique puzzle. However the tournament aspect meant you really couldn’t do this without Venompool (will be surprised if anyone places without him). The timer on the scheme was kinda mean and torpedoed our second game. Between shuffle luck for ST, MS, and useless piercing energy clogging HQ, will not be sad to never play this setup again. ;)

man, that game was about as well as it could have gone. We maxed out on the guaranteed VP coming out of the VD, which was stressful. We managed to have no escapes, at the cost of our turn count. I hope it worked out in the end though....

Since 2 of the 5 heroes were utterly useless, and fighting villains would put them in my deck, I figured I wouldn't need to recruit from the HQ much. As a result, I picked Old Man Logan, primarily for Loner, since it was an easy 4 attack. He also brought along some decent KO power to get rid of the recruit cards, once i was rolling. This also allowed Psylocke to have some use, since her 2-cost instinct card replaces itself itself, and could trigger Logan's KO.

Also brought Namor, to trigger Cabal triggers, and was able to teleport Thanos' Strength common to regularly trigger Namor's 4 cost strength card, for an easy 4 attack.He also brought more(triggerless) KO power/card draw as a bonus.

I really loved this! In Game 2 I was able to get a decent Bystander Engine going with Kid Marvel and Venompool, but it took too many turns to win. Game 1 just gave me an Early Thanos and we made quick work of Ego from there. I'd KO'd 28 bystanders by the end of the game! I'm almost sad submitting this score, honestly, because G2 felt SO MUCH BETTER. I was able to clear the trap AND the Symbiote Armor, but it just wasn't enough to outclass the few bystanders I was able to rescue/officers I could send undercover in G1 with the fast gameplay of it.

This was maniacal and frustrating. Ended up picking venompool solely to rescue bystanders and namor to ko stuff. Second game lost on a scheme twist timer which is also the worst way to lose. Did win the first but don't feel great about it.

This was probably the hardest we have ever had to chose our heroes; we did use all 3 in the end. Kitty Pride still feels like she should have been a good choice but the city we couldn't manage and our decks were just unwieldy to be any good. 
Second run Lady Thor was here and she did what was expected turning all the extra recruit into fight. 
Gamora was a powerhouse both games. The last turn of the game she drew enough cards to clear multiple villains from the city and hit Ego down to all but one hit; mark Thanos snapping away a bystander to get the last hit and rescue 3 for our only VP on the game to mark down.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Very fun setup, 'can I get a little gratitude' from Venompool went crazy against the schemes. gave me a very early tourist couple and banker rescue effects both doubled to buy Wonderman rare under the bank for free very early. The mighty were scary but also helped give villian deck info on many turns.

Red She Hulk was the MVP. I would send all but 4 attack and then I would fight her. This got me both wolverinepool cards and few other villains before she revealed a master strike and finally ended up in my victory pile.

One side focused on drawing cards and the other side focused on range cards, empowered range, and hq manipulation. 
Good game over all, but don't like Hyper-insane healing factor. If that card was not in the game it would have had a higher fun level.

This was more challenging than I expected. I leaned into the blue/green for Wonder Man's empower. Rescued a ton of bystanders due to schemes and Venompool. Second attempt went faster, with more overall VP that the first, but it seemed like the twists cane at just the wrong time. They require immediate attention, since 2 back to back starting at twist 5 means game over if you don't reduce the ants. 2 attack each isn't bad, but kept me from being able to hit Evil Deadpool as early as I wanted to. 

Decently fun, but equally as frustrating, especially with all the wounds Evil Deadpool hands out. Hoping I've done enough to advance, but I've got my doubts.

I hate Evil Deadpool but my games actually went great. First attempt I tried Carnage and Nul. I hadn’t really played Nul outside of Solo mode so I didn’t realise how much his cards can hurt the other player but after some early escapes P2 was clobbering everything with Nul’s Maul and lots of Strength draw cards. P1 was a bit of a Wound/Binding sponge but that actually helped Doc See Future Timelines and he had some good turns too. Had to shuffle Hyper-Insane Healing Factor back in no less than three times so it took 32 turns overall. Next up, I tried Stingray and Ant-Man and couldn’t have asked for a better game. Both decks got a Sting of the Stingray’s Sting in the first two turns and everything went well from there, including hitting the Healing Factor Tactic last to finish in less time, with more VP and zero escapes!
I was overrun by scheme twists on my second attempt and ended up failing. The setup was straightforward and fun!

column33: Even losing a turn from Ms. Marvel, getting early Rares made this a blowout
Very challenging. Down to the wire in both attempts.  Being able to draw cards and move villains were very important.

This one was definitely more challenging. We went with Venompool at first because of his synergy with the bystander mechanic, and seemed pretty strong on paper, however, during the game he ended up not get used much, so we was swapped out with Slapstick for the class synergy and it was a much better choice. We enjoyed the challenge of hero building in this game. The game was overall fun and challenging which is what we overall like. (P.S. FUCK Dealpool's Healing Factor!)

Snash108 built a Wonder Man deck(aka lots of ranged). Brought Slapstick along to add Ranged/Strength, as well to rescue extra bystanders using Excessive Violence.

SBPKECN focused on moving the things to the bridge and stabbing them with Nerkkod and Kuurth.
Speed run. Had some crazy combos.

Black widow saved the day. Pairing her with this scheme led to a lot of bystanders on the left. Just allowed her to excel on that side. Right went with lots of spidey and angel leading to a lot of drawing cards. Iron fist and cap added a nice amount of oomph to both hands.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: I'm honestly in shock at how well I did. I left Red Skull at Final Blow because I figured I could keep hitting villains and demons for ≥4 VP/Turn. I used a Doombot to reshuffle my Iron Fist/Cap Deck on turn 2 with one of each IF non-rare, and then drew them all on the next turn which allowed me to get a Cap uncommon and everything was smooth sailing from there. Other side went Black Widow, got an uncommon Spidey about halfway in, and thanks to early No Mercy, Doombots, and MSs, I KO'd every SHIELD card from that deck, I was drawing it (and often my whole deck) every turn. Game ended when the BW deck finally got Covert Ops, killed an Endless Armies, the following 5 demons, and the last RS in one turn. I ended up with 22/24 SHIELD cards KO'd (thanks Red Skull, BW, Doombots, and Wolvie!). I figure other people move done as well as me (this is a pretty easy setup, that screams "BRING BLACK WIDOW!"), but I enjoyed playing Legendary on easy mode for a little bit. I hope I don't regret using up 2 of the strongest heroes, but XF Wolvie and BW were too good for this setup.

Setup was so simple you had to play perfectly to have a chance of advancing. I went green and embraced wound engine, Cap+ Hulk + Colosus for wound abuse kinda worked, lets hope  it will be enough. P.S. Hydra villain group will make results check hard, with endless armies and Supreme Hydra.

I haven't played Red Skull in so long, and he is really such a pushover and hands out lollipops with his Master Strikes and his Tactics. Mentally it was a challenging setup for the tournament because I was second-guessing my hero selections and every in-game decision to make sure I wasn't missing a faster route to victory. First attempt went ok, but an HQ clogged by core Spidey led to one Goblin Queen villain escape. But there were enough glimpses of hope that the Angel/Cyclops combo would ramp up quick if I had a better HQ. I came very close to subbing in core Black Widow for my 2nd attempt given how many Bystanders were rescued via the scheme, but I didn't think the ramp would be fast enough. 2nd was much better, and the devastating combo of Angel's Drop off a Friend and Diving Catch was recruited early by one deck, and then got even better with more of those plus Cyclops's commons and uncommon. The other deck featured Cap and Iron Fist with the occasional Spidey and pulled its own. A significant amount of KO potential outside of the Hero cards helped this ramp quickly and Red Skull was vanquished (hopefully quickly enough to advance!).
After having collected so many expansion packs, I forgot how simple the core set and mastermind can be. It was nice to go back to basics for a bit.

Red Skull is very forgiving but I still felt pretty tense trying to optimize this one. VP really took off in second attempt but couldn't finish quicker without more escapes. Hero synergy was solid and really came together for me without any rares!
Scheme did not pop for us, for better or worse, allowing us to make quick work of the goblin queens. Red Skull is a very lack luster Mastermind. Did we have fun sure, but not a real challenge.

The low attack needed to kill Red Skull was deceptively easy, but the scheme included needing to prevent the "Goblin Queen" Jean Grey from escaping and inevitably all the high value Jean Grey cards are the ones that came out, making it more difficult to attack her than the mastermind.

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Pretty sure I used up every single ounce of luck that I physically and mentally possess in this game. I got a great start with gaining a handful of Thanos Cards and Venompool to rescue and KO bystanders for extra attack. Other decks was able to go almost exclusively Kingpin I got an insane top deck with one of Dr. Strangers Mastermind cards, top decking two Rares. Also got my hands on Kingpins rare card and I ended up going infinite attack with him for the win.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Namor v Namor

Our first game started a bit slow but ended up really taking off with one of us using a kingpin deck and the other raking in tons of points with ultron and green goblin. The 2nd game went horrible. After about halfway through we realized we weren't even going to get closed to winning. Dormammu still had 5 tactics left and we lost from Dr. Strange using his master strike to reveal the last plot twist.
Spider-Man is a menace.
Interesting game.  Had a tough time building up enough attack to take out all of the tactics until Thanos' ultimate came out.

That was a great thematic setup! My plan was to blitz Dormammu before he could get more tactics but both of my games had 4 Scheme Twists in the first 6 VD cards. Oh well. I knew going in I wanted Sabretooth, so I chose Old Man Logan to complement him and Carnage. I had plenty of KOing on the Instinct side which led to me getting a hit on the MM every left hand turn. The Kingpin/Thanos side kept the city clear while I used Thanos' rare to build up bystander KOs for MM hits. Unfortunately, both hands took too long to get their engines going so my high turn count reflects that. Very fun games though. My first game Black Cat sit in the city the whole game but game 2 I was able to take her out twice so that felt like a small victory since her Elusive 6 makes her tricky to fight.

Both games lasted the exact number of turns. Game two actually had Kingpin go infinite, but  with two escapes it didn't make the cut for submission even though it was 0.2 PPT more. 

Game 1 had a better distribution of the Villain Deck, but only one side was able to effectively thin and Kingpin was very late to the party. So Carnage did a bulk of the work. 

Game 2 was ultimately a more fun game (I'd give it a 4 on its own, because the Bystander mechanic definitely felt more useful than Captain Marvel ended up being). 

Both games having all 4 Drained Tactics coming in really made for a long game and the Demonic Bargains usually felt bad on one side or the other and with minimal KO power the Wounds were frustrating; especially in the second playthrough. 

Overall, I continue to like Carnage. Venompool surprised me (Thank god Shenanigans only came out a few times and was pretty quickly KOd by Enchantress to get recruit. Kingpin is always fun when he shows up. Thanos would have been but he was pretty useless these go-arounds.  Captain Marvel is a absolutely solid hero set imo, but the lack of her draw common was unfortunate.  Alright - I think I've talked enough. Thank you for coming, have a nice day!

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Fun game, it just took a long time, which will likely hurt my chances, so I’m hoping it took others in my pod a long time too. I dedicated one player to Thanos and his bystander KO engine and the other to Kingpin and his New Recruit engine. The other 3 characters supported those 2 nicely. It just took a bit of time to build up to big numbers. Thankfully, I got lucky and got Utter Annihilation somewhat early, so I was able to draw that multiple times.

First game with FF synergy did not work at all. Way too much recruit and no attack. Second game went beautifully and was able to whittle the decks down to pure heroes (no shield) and get most of each deck in hand each turn. First tactic won on turn 13 and then final blow on turn 18. Hoping that’s faster than most, but my escapes won’t help much.
Two games were very different.  The first I was able to keep my city clean and no one escaped.  The second, due to a run of twists, several villains were able to get away.  Very enjoyable game
It was difficult to prevent any villains from escaping. It would’ve been fun to use some other expansions
got unlucky at the start then my engine kicked in
Had to go with raw power in my character choices this week so hopefully I don't lose out of the tournament
The second game I was able to get my hands up with some card draw and some attack so I had a quicker time of things than game 1 which took 7 more turns.
A lot of enemies coming out the deck!

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Lost a few good cards to Doombots which slowed me down. My wife went for Thing early which paid off with a lot of recruit! Between us we bought 3 Rares. Chaining Twists early led to 3 escapes but from turn 8 we were back on track with the City under control. Doubt we were quick enough to trouble the top of the table though!
This Scheme was no fun! We like to keep our hero’s we recruit. Our HQ wasn’t the greatest starting out but we made it through with a win.
Colossus made for some interesting wound cycling. Very fun!

This one was pushing the limits on the fun scale. I lost my first attempt despite having 2 disappearing acts in HQ, so 1 for each Hand/Deck. THose freaking Scheme Twists Mutated them away from their usefulness EVERY SINGLE TIME. I didn't get close to winning at all in my first attempt, I even thought that it went pretty well because of the difficult setup. but I could get in my second attempt right after and boy was I surprised by the difference in luck/shuffle. First off, a Scheme Twist on turn 1, we can call that a win with this one. Phalanx on turn 3 and 4 if I recall correctly, making the early Trooper K.O. for both Decks. I included Forge in my setup, both for the chance of his Rare helping out (which, spoiler, it really friggin' did!), but also to create a chance of defeating Phalanx later on in the game without having to K.O. better heroes because all of his carrds are Tech.

Deckbuilding wise the second attempt was skewed hugely in favor of Hand 2, which got disappearing act to work beautifully and very early on. My deck was 8 or 9 cards for a long time. I ended up being able to buy Forge's rare AND able to trigger it almost every turn (first time around it didn't work yet, but with 3 other Tech cards in a deck of 8/9 it wasn't that hard (2 of those were the discard 1 to draw 2, so I could reliably draw almost all my cards. if not all my cards). Hand 1 had a rough game, the schem twists were harder for deck 1 than deck 2, with also gaining a wound from Colossus's 1 cost. 20 points was in hindsight pretty good for a deck that could make at most 10 attack at one point.

The difficulty was also in the setup. Red King Transforming from a "Clear the city or I won't let you fight me" and Masterstrike "Here, have a wound while I gloat at you" into a "If you don't beat me before I Master Strike again I'll play another card from the Villain Deck and hide behind them and if you DO defeat me first my Tactics won't be good for you!" Without Forge's rare beating the guy twice I don't think I would have made it. Perhaps there was a better chance with different hero choices, but this felt right for me, although the first attempt went really badly.

I hope I'm still in it for next week. Good luck to you all and see your results soon!

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: I never knew wounds could be SO FUN! We brought in gladiator hulk and his son Skar to embrace the joys of wounded fury. Between all the heroes, there were no tech cards so we could embrace further wounds from the master strikes. Near the end, we clocked up some serious hulk damage! Hulk smash indeed! Great set up!

Mockingbird and Deathlok were the GOATs! P1 was sending every SHIELD person undercover who showed up. Managed to pick up Deathlok rare early and was getting double digit attack and recruit every 3 turns with how thin my deck was. Had to mutate a to Grey Hulk'd Threaten and Bribe card, letting me burn stars for attack. It's not often that I feel like recruiting will hurt my deck, but that did happen. P1 hit the MM for all 4 tactics before P2 took the final blow. P2 went with the Colossus and Thing deck, while we both used Brood Queen as twist and KO fodder. P2 wasn't able to get enough of the wound block cards early enough but was able to feast some of the wounds away. Shield bros really had more fun, but final blow came from a Thing rare and Colossus rare combo. Game felt good and like we were in control the whole time. Game 2, not so much.

This setup is more annoying than difficult, with so many cards that can mess up your whole turn in the Villain Deck. Also needing to have a clear city on Red King's default side can get frustrating, and the pressure to hit him increases as the game gets near the end.

Am I just cosmicly unlucky or do I suck? The world may never know. Lost both matches. First try, over half the villain deck was still there so I wasn't really able o evaluate my hero choices. Second try both decks started to click, but I'd have a good hand and then I'd lose it to a scheme twist and I could draw a MS so I was stuck with Red King on his hard side.

real tough, didnt get much going and after the first game was a loss i did not know what heroes to pivot into after learning they both didnt perform well for me, the swap didnt make a difference leading to an even worse game 2.

My two attempts were so similar, hope I submitted the better one! F4 United is such a fun hero. Lots of focusing in both attempts, just couldn't get going too quickly either time unfortunately.

Second Round: Many unlucky Scheme-Twists, which couldn't be resolved.

Falcon was a very useful pick. We had many ranged combos.

This is a fun scheme. I tried to go for some cohesion with the FF, and it worked, just not well enough. With Ragnarok usually at +6 or +8, I kept finding myself just out of range of hitting him. I got very lucky on some VD action (Fenris Wolf on turn 1 was excellent!), just couldn’t get my deck thin enough to see all the good cards together. Tried to go recruit heavy with focus on one side. Thought I could get cheeky with Psi-Lord’s VD manipulation, but unfortunately never worked out the way I wanted. See ya in the lower lower bracket!

Both games were kinda rushed I didn't really have time to play during my weekend so I had to fit them in after work but I got them done. Hopefully I make it through though I imagine the whole pod will probably win and the points will be higher with Mysterio.

column33: Playing against Mysterio was a lot of fun. His tactics and strikes were very thematic. I chose two heroes with access to green cards to assist with the scheme twists and it did come in handy cancelling out several KOs throughout the game. Moon Knights rare did tons of work grabbing bystanders but overall Iron Fist and Domino felt most impactful. Other than one villain that was pushed out of the city with an ambush, the game plan felt like it worked being super aggressive against Mysterio once attack cards allowed.
Tougher than I expected, won on the last card.
I played the wrong setup, please forfeit my hand.  I played pod 15's setup for no reason and had bad luck with scheme twists in that one anyhow.  Looking forward to next tournament!
